full_name: Service Alberta and Red Tape Reduction
blurb: Provides Albertans with access to essential registry and identity services, protects consumers in the marketplace, and leads government-wide efforts to reduce red tape and improve digital service delivery.

goals.title: OUTCOME 1: Albertans can access essential registry and identity services
goals.description: The ministry provides Albertans with secure access to vital records, driver's licences, vehicle registration, and business registry services. Through a network of registry agents and online channels, Albertans can obtain documents and register important life events.

foundational_services.name: Operate and oversee Alberta's registry agent network
foundational_services.desc: Manages the provincial network of registry agents located in service centres, municipalities, and private service providers who deliver registry services on behalf of the government. Ensures consistent service quality, training, and access across the province.
foundational_services.users: Registry agents, municipal staff, service centre operators

supporting.name: Register a birth or get a birth certificate
supporting.desc: Parents and guardians can register a newborn's birth or obtain a birth certificate. Birth registration is a critical identity document required for school enrolment, passport applications, and other government services.
supporting.users: New parents, guardians, individuals needing birth certificates

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of thousands of birth registrations annually
reach_estimate.basis: All births in Alberta must be registered within 30 days

economic_impact.note: Birth registration enables access to education, health services, and other government programs

supporting.name: Get a marriage certificate or register a marriage
supporting.desc: Couples can register a marriage and obtain marriage certificates. Marriage registration is required for legal recognition of marriage and is needed for various government, financial, and personal purposes.

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of thousands of marriage registrations annually
reach_estimate.basis: All marriages in Alberta must be registered

economic_impact.note: Marriage registration enables spousal benefits, property rights, and legal recognition

supporting.name: Get a death certificate
supporting.desc: Next of kin and legal representatives can obtain death certificates. Death certificates are required for estate administration, insurance claims, pension benefits, and legal matters following a death.

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of death certificates issued annually
reach_estimate.basis: Death certificates required for all deaths registered in Alberta

economic_impact.note: Death certificates enable estate settlement and access to survivor benefits

main_services.name: Get a driver's licence or register a vehicle

supporting.name: Apply for or renew your driver's licence
supporting.desc: Albertans can apply for a new driver's licence, renew an existing licence, or request a duplicate. Driver's licences serve as government-issued identity documents and authorization to operate vehicles on Alberta roads.
supporting.users: Residents, new drivers, licence renewal applicants

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of driver's licence transactions annually
reach_estimate.basis: Alberta has millions of licensed drivers with regular renewal requirements

economic_impact.note: Driver's licences enable employment, travel, and identity verification

supporting.name: Register or renew your vehicle registration
supporting.desc: Vehicle owners can register a new vehicle, renew registration, or make changes to vehicle registration. Vehicle registration is required to legally operate a vehicle in Alberta and is needed for insurance and roadside identification.
supporting.users: Vehicle owners, fleet operators, new vehicle buyers

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of vehicle registrations annually
reach_estimate.basis: All vehicles operated in Alberta must be registered

economic_impact.note: Vehicle registration enables legal road operation and supports insurance requirements

supporting.name: Get a learner's permit or upgrade your licence class
supporting.desc: New drivers can obtain a learner's permit to practice driving, and experienced drivers can upgrade to a higher licence class for commercial or specialized driving. The ministry manages Alberta's graduated licensing system.
supporting.users: New drivers, drivers seeking to upgrade licence class

channels.label: Written and driving tests at registry locations

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of thousands of learner's permits and licence upgrades annually
reach_estimate.basis: New drivers and commercial drivers require learner permits and upgraded classes

economic_impact.note: Graduated licensing supports safe driver development and commercial transportation

main_services.name: Access registry services for businesses and personal property

supporting.name: Register a business name or corporation in Alberta
supporting.desc: Entrepreneurs can register a sole proprietorship business name, partnership, or incorporate a business in Alberta. Business registration establishes legal identity and is required to operate a business, hire employees, and access business services.

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of thousands of business registrations annually
reach_estimate.basis: All new businesses and corporations must register in Alberta

economic_impact.note: Business registration enables entrepreneurship, employment creation, and economic growth

supporting.name: Search or register a personal property lien
supporting.desc: Creditors and lenders can register security interests in personal property, and searchers can look up registered liens. The Personal Property Registry is a searchable public database of security interests on vehicles, equipment, and other personal property.

relationships.service: Register, search, or change a personal property lien

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of liens registered and searched annually
reach_estimate.basis: Lenders routinely search and register security interests in personal property

economic_impact.note: Transparent lien registry enables secure lending and protects creditor rights

goals.title: OUTCOME 2: Albertans are protected as consumers
goals.description: The ministry protects Albertans from unfair marketplace practices, helps resolve consumer disputes, and enforces consumer protection laws. The ministry investigates complaints, educates consumers about their rights, and takes action against non-compliant businesses.

foundational_services.name: Develop consumer protection policy and enforce marketplace rules
foundational_services.desc: Develops and updates consumer protection legislation and policy to address emerging marketplace issues, unfair trade practices, and new business models. Enforces compliance through investigation and prosecution of violations.
foundational_services.users: Government policy makers, enforcement staff, legal teams

relationships.service: File a complaint about a business or marketplace issue
Check for consumer alerts and enforcement actions
File a condominium dispute for resolution

main_services.name: File a consumer complaint or learn about your rights

supporting.name: File a complaint about a business or marketplace issue
supporting.desc: Consumers can file complaints about unfair business practices, fraudulent transactions, product defects, or service failures. The ministry investigates complaints, mediates disputes, and takes enforcement action against non-compliant businesses.

relationships.service: Develop consumer protection policy and enforce marketplace rules

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of thousands of consumer complaints annually
reach_estimate.basis: Consumer complaints span retail, online sales, services, and digital transactions

economic_impact.note: Complaint mechanisms protect consumer rights and deter unfair business practices

supporting.name: Check for consumer alerts and enforcement actions
supporting.desc: Albertans can check for active consumer alerts about problematic businesses, scams, or unsafe products. The ministry publishes enforcement actions and warnings to protect consumers from known risks.

relationships.service: Develop consumer protection policy and enforce marketplace rules

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of Albertans access consumer alerts
reach_estimate.basis: Consumer alerts reach broad audiences through web and social media

economic_impact.note: Alerts inform consumers and reduce fraud losses and unsafe product exposure

supporting.name: File a condominium dispute for resolution
supporting.desc: Condominium owners and corporations can file disputes with the ministry for mediation and resolution. Common disputes include shared property maintenance, special assessments, bylaw violations, and governance issues. The ministry provides dispute resolution services.
supporting.users: Condominium owners, condo boards, property managers

relationships.service: Develop consumer protection policy and enforce marketplace rules

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of condominium dispute filings annually
reach_estimate.basis: Alberta has tens of thousands of condominium units with ongoing governance

economic_impact.note: Dispute resolution services protect condo property values and owner rights

goals.title: OUTCOME 3: Government services are accessible and red tape is reduced
goals.description: The ministry leads government-wide efforts to reduce regulatory burden, improve digital service delivery, and ensure Albertans can easily access government information and assert their privacy rights.

foundational_services.name: Lead red tape reduction and digital service delivery across government
foundational_services.desc: Coordinates government-wide red tape reduction initiatives, digital transformation, and service improvement efforts. Develops standards for digital service delivery and works with ministries to eliminate unnecessary requirements.
foundational_services.users: Government policy makers, digital transformation teams, service improvement leads

channels.label: Red tape reduction and service improvement

main_services.name: Access government information and privacy rights

supporting.name: Submit a freedom of information request
supporting.desc: Albertans can request government records and information through the Freedom of Information process. Requests provide access to government documents, meeting minutes, policy files, and other public records subject to privacy and security exceptions.

legislation.full_name: Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of FOIP requests filed annually
reach_estimate.basis: Media, researchers, and citizens regularly request government documents

economic_impact.note: FOIP access supports government accountability and informed public discourse

supporting.name: File a privacy complaint
supporting.desc: Albertans can file complaints if they believe their personal information has been improperly collected, used, or disclosed by government or businesses. The ministry investigates privacy violations and enforces privacy protections.
supporting.users: Individuals whose privacy may have been violated

legislation.full_name: Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act

reach_estimate.basis: Privacy complaints arise from government and private sector handling of personal data

economic_impact.note: Privacy enforcement protects individuals from unauthorized data use and identity risks
